headline: Apple’s MotionVFX Deal Deepens Creator Studio And Services Ecosystem
summary: Apple has acquired MotionVFX, a developer of plug ins and tools for Final Cut Pro, in a move to deepen its creative software offering. The deal is aimed at building out the Creator Studio bundle and reinforcing Apple's position with professional content creators. The acquisition ties into Apple's wider focus on Services and subscriptions as part of its broader ecosystem strategy. NasdaqGS:AAPL currently trades at $254.23, with the share price reflecting a 20.1% return over the past year and...

headline: Electronics firm Jabil raises annual forecasts on data center demand
summary: Electronic component maker Jabil raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts on Wednesday, ‌betting on strong demand for infrastructure services from ‌AI data centers. Shares of the St. Petersburg, Florida-based company were up 1.1% ​in premarket trading, after it surpassed Wall Street expectations for second-quarter results. A surge in spending for data-center infrastructure, driven by robust demand for computing power to support AI ‌technologies, has benefited ⁠companies such as Jabil.

headline: How Investors May Respond To QUALCOMM (QCOM) $20 Billion Buyback And Higher Dividend
summary: In March 2026, Qualcomm’s board approved a new US$20.00 billion share repurchase program with no expiration date and raised the quarterly cash dividend from US$0.89 to US$0.92 per share, lifting the annualized payout to US$3.68. headline: How The Apple (AAPL) Investment Narrative Is Shifting With AI, Costs And Regional Demand
summary: Apple’s analyst fair value target has moved from US$291.65 to US$295.44 per share, a modest adjustment that still matters if you are tracking where the stock might be fairly priced. That shift lines up with recent research that credits new products, services and AI efforts on one side, and rising component costs and uneven regional demand on the other. Read on to see how these mixed views are shaping the evolving Apple story and how you can keep up with it. Stay updated as the Fair Value for...

headline: Trump’s tariffs are hurting American manufacturers instead of helping them
summary: Jay Allen is a fan of President Donald Trump, and voted for him on the belief that the Republican would cut taxes and trim regulations, helping his manufacturing business in northeast Arkansas.  The import taxes have raised the costs of engines, steel, gearboxes and clutches made abroad that Allen needs to build power trowels that can sell for up to $100,000 each.  Allen’s experience embodies a growing body of evidence that the tariffs that Trump said would help American factories are, in fact, squashing many of them.

content.title: Trump’s tariffs are hurting American manufacturers instead of helping them
content.summary: Jay Allen is a fan of President Donald Trump, and voted for him on the belief that the Republican would cut taxes and trim regulations, helping his manufacturing business in northeast Arkansas.  The import taxes have raised the costs of engines, steel, gearboxes and clutches made abroad that Allen needs to build power trowels that can sell for up to $100,000 each.  Allen’s experience embodies a growing body of evidence that the tariffs that Trump said would help American factories are, in fact, squashing many of them.

thumbnail.caption: A welder is seen inside the Allen Engineering Corporation plant Monday, March 16, 2026, in Paragould, Ark. (AP Photo/Kevin Wurm)
